title: Apartments Near Cleveland Clinic
metaTitle: Apartments Near Cleveland Clinic Main Campus
metaDescription: Looking for apartments near Cleveland Clinic's main campus? See nearby east-side neighborhoods, transit options, rents, and voucher-friendly rental homes.
secondaryKeywords: apartments near cleveland clinic main campus
answerBox: Cleveland Clinic's main campus sits in the Fairfax neighborhood on Cleveland's east side, adjacent to University Circle and served by the HealthLine bus rapid transit route on Euclid Avenue. Renters looking for housing near the Clinic often consider Fairfax (ZIP 44104) and neighboring east-side areas, where Rent Finder Cleveland works with Section 8-accepting rental homes.

sections.h2: Where is Cleveland Clinic's main campus?
body: Cleveland Clinic's main campus is in **Fairfax**, an east-side Cleveland neighborhood in **ZIP code 44104**, immediately adjacent to University Circle. Fairfax sits along the Euclid Avenue corridor served by the **HealthLine**, GCRTA's bus rapid transit route connecting Public Square downtown, the Midtown medical corridor, University Circle, and East Cleveland. Cleveland Clinic and University Hospitals together are the region's two largest employers, so demand for nearby housing comes heavily from medical staff, residents, and support workers on varied shift schedules.

Housing directly around the main campus ranges from larger apartment buildings to older single-family homes and duplexes converted to rentals. Fairfax borders **Central**, an east-side neighborhood just southeast of downtown with its own long housing history, and **Hough** to the north, both within a short distance of the Clinic's campus. Renters who want more room than a typical studio, or who are relocating with a household, often look slightly beyond the immediate campus blocks into these surrounding east-side neighborhoods, where the rental stock leans toward older single-family homes, duplexes, and small multi-family buildings rather than large new construction.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent near Cleveland Clinic?
body: Rents close to a major medical campus can run above the citywide figures because of steady demand. For general market context, here's what two widely used rent-tracking sources reported citywide: Zumper's median-asking-rent report (dated July 4, 2026) and RentCafe's average-rent report for professionally managed apartments (dated July 2, 2026).

sections.h2: Getting to the Clinic without a car
body: The HealthLine's dedicated center-median busway makes it one of the more reliable transit options for shift workers commuting to Cleveland Clinic or University Hospitals at off-peak hours, since it runs in its own lane separate from general traffic along much of Euclid Avenue. The RTA Red Line, Cleveland's heavy-rail line, also serves the east side and connects to Tower City downtown, where riders can transfer to the Blue and Green light-rail lines toward Shaker Heights. Beyond rail and the HealthLine, GCRTA also runs a broader network of local bus routes across the east side, and paratransit service is available separately. Anyone commuting on a rotating shift schedule should confirm current GCRTA service hours directly with the agency, since frequency can drop late at night on some routes.

sections.h2: Voucher-friendly rental homes near the Clinic
body: Rent Finder Cleveland works with rental homes in **Fairfax and the neighboring Central neighborhood (ZIP 44104)**, right around Cleveland Clinic's main campus, as well as in nearby **Hough (44103)** just to the north along the same Euclid Avenue corridor. Every home we work with in this area accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready — a relevant option for hospital support staff, traveling contract workers, or anyone whose income includes a voucher.

See our [Fairfax neighborhood page](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-fairfax-cleveland/) for more on the immediate area, or [book a showing](/p/book-showing) to ask what's currently available near the Clinic. Our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) explains how voucher acceptance works locally.

faq.q: What neighborhood is Cleveland Clinic's main campus in?
faq.a: Cleveland Clinic's main campus is in Fairfax, ZIP code 44104, on Cleveland's east side adjacent to University Circle. The area is served by the HealthLine bus rapid transit route along Euclid Avenue and is near the RTA Red Line.

faq.q: Is there public transit to Cleveland Clinic?
faq.a: Yes. The HealthLine runs a dedicated busway along Euclid Avenue connecting Public Square, the Midtown medical corridor, University Circle, and East Cleveland. The RTA Red Line also serves the east side, with all rail lines meeting at Tower City downtown. Confirm current schedules with GCRTA, especially for night-shift commutes.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 apartments near Cleveland Clinic?
faq.a: Rent Finder Cleveland works with Section 8-accepting rental homes in Fairfax and Central (44104), right around the Clinic's main campus, and in nearby Hough (44103). Availability changes regularly, so book a showing to ask what's currently open.

faq.q: How much does an apartment near Cleveland Clinic cost?
faq.a: Rents vary by building. Citywide, per Zumper's July 2026 report, a 1-bedroom runs about $1,195 and a 2-bedroom about $1,100 in median asking rent; per RentCafe's July 2026 report, professionally managed apartment averages run notably higher. Areas immediately around the Clinic can price above these citywide figures.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland offer short-term housing for traveling medical staff?
faq.a: Our standard rental homes are typically leased on regular terms rather than nightly or weekly stays. If you need housing for a temporary assignment near the Clinic, book a showing and describe your timeline so our team can tell you what options, if any, might fit.

faq.q: What neighborhoods border Cleveland Clinic's main campus?
faq.a: Fairfax, where the main campus sits, borders Central to the southwest and Hough to the north, and is adjacent to University Circle to the east. All are within a short distance of the campus along or near the Euclid Avenue transit corridor, with rental stock leaning toward older homes, duplexes, and small multi-family buildings.
